task_completion.description: Import the camera stations from C:\workspace\data\camera_stations.gpx
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application shows 5 waypoints/camera stations loaded onto the map or listed in the sidebar.

task_completion.description: Create a route connecting all 5 stations
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible route line on the map that connects all 5 imported camera station waypoints.

task_completion.description: Optimize the route for the shortest travel path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The route connecting the 5 points appears visually efficient, forming a logical sequence without obvious crisscrossing or overlapping lines.
The text 'Optimized Patrol' is visible in the route's name field, properties dialog, or sidebar list.
A 'Save As' or 'Export' dialog showing the file being saved as 'optimized_patrol.gpx' in the 'C:\workspace' directory, or a file explorer window showing the newly created file in that location.

integrity.description: Agent used a graphical mapping or GIS application to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI application (like QGIS, Google Earth, Garmin BaseCamp, etc.) being used, rather than terminal commands or a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually type or paste GPX XML code to bypass the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The route creation and export were done via the application's built-in tools and menus, not by editing a .gpx file in Notepad or VS Code.

integrity.description: Agent actually created and optimized the route visually or via a tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacted with route creation/optimization tools or manually ordered the points in the GUI, rather than running a pre-written script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly 'C:\workspace\optimized_patrol.gpx'. The route name must be exactly 'Optimized Patrol'. The route must connect exactly 5 points. A properly optimized route will have a travel distance under 8.0 km (theoretical minimum is ~2.97 km), which visually means the route should not crisscross itself.
